name: La Via Verde d'Alcoi – Gravel circular at the Parque Natural de la Font Roja
name_original: La Vía Verde d'Alcoi – Gravel circular al Parque Natural de la Font Roja
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alcoi offers diverse landscapes, with 70% of its territory covered by the Font Roja Natural Park and the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park. The region features an extensive network of routes, including the Vía Verde de Alcoy, which follows an old railway line through illuminated tunnels and over viaducts. Cyclists can explore Mediterranean mixed forests, areas known for aromatic herbs and springs, and dramatic cliffs. The terrain ranges from mostly paved greenways to challenging paths with significant elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Alcoi The most popular touring cycling route is La Via Verde d'Alcoi – Gravel circular at the Parque Natural de la Font Roja, a 22.5 miles (36.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Font Roja Natural Park on a gravel path. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alcoi Greenway Tunnel – Alcoy Greenway loop from Alcoi, a moderate 19.5 miles (31.4 km) path. This route follows the Vía Verde, passing through tunnels and over viaducts, offering accessible terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Carrasqueta Pass – Alcoy Greenway loop from Alcoi, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail leading through pine and holm oak forests, often completed in about 4 hours 29 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcoi is defined by natural parks like Font Roja and Sierra de Mariola, old railway lines converted into greenways, and varied forest landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenway sections to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Alcoi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcoi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Alcoi?
A: Alcoi offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 60 different options available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Alcoi?
A: The terrain around Alcoi is quite diverse. You'll find routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5300211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcoi Greenway Tunnel – Alcoy Greenway loop from Alcoi&lt;/a&gt; that follow old railway lines, offering mostly paved or well-maintained dirt paths suitable for families and beginners. Other routes, particularly those venturing into the Font Roja Natural Park or Sierra de Mariola, can be more challenging with significant elevation changes and gravel sections, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952670008/la-via-verde-d-alcoi-gravel-circular-at-the-parque-natural-de-la-font-roja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Via Verde d'Alcoi – Gravel circular at the Parque Natural de la Font Roja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Alcoi?
A: Alcoi is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly recommended, offering mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially in autumn when the Font Roja Natural Park displays vibrant ochre and reddish hues. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler days.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Alcoi?
A: Yes, Alcoi is home to the Vía Verde de Alcoy, which is ideal for families and those seeking an easier ride. This 10-kilometer (one-way) route follows an old railway line, passing through illuminated tunnels and over impressive viaducts. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5300211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcoi Greenway Tunnel – Alcoy Greenway loop from Alcoi&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that utilizes this accessible greenway.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Alcoi?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes in Alcoi are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952670008/la-via-verde-d-alcoi-gravel-circular-at-the-parque-natural-de-la-font-roja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Via Verde d'Alcoi – Gravel circular at the Parque Natural de la Font Roja&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40341285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass – Alcoy Greenway loop from Alcoi&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views through pine and holm oak forests.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Alcoi's routes are rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can cycle through the Mediterranean mixed forests of the Font Roja Natural Park and the aromatic herb-filled landscapes of the Sierra de Mariola. The Vía Verde de Alcoy features 11 illuminated tunnels and three viaducts, including the impressive Viaducto de las Siete Lunas. For historical sights, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31901838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de Alcoy – Views of Barxell Castle loop from Alcoi&lt;/a&gt; offer glimpses of structures like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3097812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barchell Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3621301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castalla Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4157105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covetes dels Moros&lt;/a&gt; caves.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer great viewpoints?
A: Many routes in Alcoi provide excellent viewpoints, especially those that climb to higher elevations. The Font Roja Natural Park has numerous viewpoints offering panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40341285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass – Alcoy Greenway loop from Alcoi&lt;/a&gt; is known for its picturesque views of pine and holm oak forests, and even expansive coastal panoramas on clear days. You can also find a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6586349" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cocentaina Castle&lt;/a&gt; along some routes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alcoi?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alcoi are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained Vía Verde de Alcoy with its unique tunnels and viaducts, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs through natural parks. The variety of routes, from easy greenways to more demanding trails, is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.

Q: Is it possible to access Alcoi's cycling routes using public transport?
A: Alcoi is a city with public transport connections. While specific routes may require a short ride or walk from a public transport stop, the Vía Verde de Alcoy, for instance, is easily accessible from the city center. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and bike transport policies if you plan to use public transport to reach trailheads.

Q: Are there parking facilities available near the cycling routes in Alcoi?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Alcoi, especially near popular access points for the Vía Verde and other trailheads. Many routes start from or pass through the city, where public parking options exist. For routes venturing into the natural parks, designated parking areas are usually provided at visitor centers or main access points.

Q: Can I cycle with my dog on the touring routes in Alcoi?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's important to check specific regulations for the natural parks (Font Roja and Sierra de Mariola) regarding dogs, especially if they need to be on a leash. Greenways like the Vía Verde de Alcoy are generally suitable for cycling with dogs, provided they are well-behaved and kept under control. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain.

Q: Are there any routes that connect to other cycling networks?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde de Alcoy is a significant part of the local cycling infrastructure and is even integrated into the EuroVelo 8 long-distance cycling route. It also connects to the Cocentaina Greenway, further expanding the network of safe cycling paths and offering opportunities for longer, multi-day touring adventures beyond Alcoi itself.
tips: The greenway runs along the old railway line that linked Alcoy with Alicante and has now been refurbished for cyclists and hikers. Just over 8 km long and 90% paved, the route is very picturesque and entertaining. There are several tunnels to cross, some of which are several hundred metres long and are illuminated. An ideal route for the whole family.
